title: Nightly Improver runner + memory-consolidation rule (proposal diff + queue) + scheduler registration; vault-locked/server-down skip

tests.summary: 11 passed — runImprover produces a memory-consolidation proposal from MEMORY.md learned hints (claim + evidence + real diff + one decision); no hints → no proposal; upsertQueue idempotent by id; vault-locked + server-down → records skip (no proposals); CLI run-now writes a proposal artifact + a pending review-queue entry (improver-proposal-ok); CLI run-now with locked vault → {skipped:'vault locked'} (improver-skip-ok). Full suite 612 passed / 9 skipped / 0 failed.

scheduled.summary: improver-scheduled-ok — scheduler list shows improver-nightly @ cron '30 3 * * *', enabled. Registered via setup.sh (positional `add <id> <cron> <command>`, morning-briefing pattern).

e2e.summary: N/A — nightly runner + proposal core; committed CLI + unit tests are the gate. The review-queue own-port UI + LIVE hosted-API apply is deferred (see blocker).

by: N/A — backend runner; review-queue UI deferred

blockers: improver-apply-ok / improver-reject-ok / improver-conflict-ok (Approve in the review UI → applied via hosted API → reconcile; Reject untouched; 409 on concurrent claude-md edit) are DEFERRED: they require the review-queue own-port UI + the live Next server + the hosted authoring APIs. The apply PATH is fully specified (POST /api/quarters file.* [owned-vs-loose via promote/park], PUT /api/claude-md with baselineSha→409, PUT /api/settings, the router's PUT /routing) and its dependencies all exist + reconcile('post-authoring') is already wired (MR0c). The own-port review-queue view + the live apply round-trip is the remaining UI integration. Logged, not silently dropped.
notes: improver-core.mjs: the nightly run reads decisions.jsonl + MEMORY.md (learned hints), produces a memory-consolidation PROPOSAL (the presentation standard: plain-language claim + evidence + a diff + ONE decision), upserts a review-queue index (idempotent by id), and writes a run report. Needs NO HTTP to RUN; APPLY happens only through hosted APIs from the review UI (the core never writes owned surfaces directly). Vault-locked / server-down → records `skipped` (never fails silently). Seed: the one working end-to-end rule (memory consolidation); skill/automation/router-config/garrison rules ship as disabled examples (incl. a garrison pool-tuning rule wired to decisions.jsonl + pool stats — documented in the fitting). RETRY 1: multi-line .mjs import tripped TS7016 → single-line. Satisfies brief §3 tokens improver-proposal-ok, improver-skip-ok, improver-scheduled-ok.
